title: schnorr.rs: Nonce derivation uses privkey directly instead of RFC 8032 prefix
description:
File: rust/lichen-link/src/schnorr.rs
Lines: 51-56

The sign function derives the nonce as:

    let nonce_hash = Sha512::new()
        .chain_update(privkey.as_bytes())
        .chain_update(msg)
        .finalize();
    let r = Scalar::from_bytes_mod_order_wide(&nonce_hash.into());

This uses the clamped private scalar (32 bytes) directly in the nonce derivation.

Standard Ed25519 (RFC 8032) uses:
    r = H(prefix || msg) where prefix = H(seed)[32:64]

The difference:
- RFC 8032: Uses the second half of H(seed), never the scalar itself
- schnorr.rs: Uses the clamped scalar derived from first half of H(seed)

While both are deterministic and secure, using the actual private scalar in the hash input is non-standard. If there is ever a hash collision or related-message attack, the attacker gains information about the private scalar directly rather than the nonce prefix.

This is the same issue as project-LICHEN-z8r8 but in the Rust implementation.

Impact: Non-standard nonce derivation. Standard Ed25519 security proofs may not apply directly. Should be documented as an intentional design deviation or changed to match RFC 8032.
